rows.notes: Top-line entry with clear numeric alignment (8 16 8) using the total row as column guide. The description handwriting is partially ambiguous (letters read as 'Trehaven'); chosen reading is plausible but uncertain due to cursive flourishes.
Description reads clearly as 'Morton Vicarage'. No aligned numbers in the pounds/shillings/pence columns for this row (blank). Vertical column boundaries established from total row confirm emptiness.
Handwritten list of names; reading 'Le Blanc, Smyth, Moreton Annie' is the best fit to the cursive on the page but some letters are indistinct. No amounts present in the aligned columns.
Description interpreted as 'Mordenham force' (handwriting slightly unclear). No monetary values aligned to this line when using the total row's column positions.

Pounds column shows a '4' which is struck through with a vertical stroke (visible). Transcribed the '4' but flagged strike-through in the notes; shillings and pence clearly read as 0 and 0. Column alignment confirmed using the total row.

Pounds shows a struck-through '4' (vertical cancel) — transcribed as '4' and noted as struck-through. Shillings/pence read as 0/0. Column positions taken from the total row; strike-through is visually evident.
Description is somewhat ambiguous in the cursive; read as 'Port & Fairy & Modernham' as best fit. No amounts visible in the aligned columns. Slight page tilt and faded ink reduce confidence.

rows.description: of Mr Holsworthy from an unknown hand
rows.notes: Description legible as 'of Mr Holsworthy from an unknown hand'. Numeric columns clearly show 5 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence when aligned to the total row boundaries. Very clear compared to other lines.
This appears to be a section heading (different handwriting and spacing). Text reads approximately 'Quercworth & Battley' though letters are faded; no amounts in the aligned columns.
Short label beneath the section heading; read as 'Gent. Comd?' (the abbreviation is unclear). No monetary values aligned to this line.

This row shows a long dash in the pounds column instead of figures (visible horizontal dash across the numeric area). Per page guidance this dash is interpreted as an empty/zero monetary value; columns checked against the total-row alignment to confirm there are no hidden digits.

Description includes a superscript 'of' (transcribed here as 'Con of Library' per page guidance). No amounts visible when aligned to the total-row column boundaries; there is a small central scribble but no aligned monetary figures.

Small centered pencilled/cursive note adjacent to the Con/Library line reads like 'Sum?' or 'Sund' — taken as a centered header/annotation. Fading and smudge reduce confidence; no amounts adjacent except the final total below.
Blank/underline row preceding the final total. The underline is visible but contains no description text. Included to preserve ordering before the total row; heavy fading here reduces legibility.

Final total clearly written as '210 02 : 06q' (q interpreted as a farthing = 0.25). Column boundaries for pounds/shillings/pence were established from this row and applied to all above. Very clear and used as the primary reference per page-specific guidance.
